scenes.concept: Introduction to Newton's Laws of Motion
scenes.visual: Display the title 'Newton's Laws of Motion' centered on screen with three labeled boxes below it numbered 1, 2, and 3, each representing one law.

Show a stationary circle on the left side of the screen labeled 'Object at Rest' that remains still. Next to it, show a circle moving horizontally at constant speed labeled 'Object in Motion'. Then animate an arrow labeled 'External Force' pushing the moving circle, causing it to accelerate.

Display the formula 'F = m × a' prominently. Show two squares of different sizes labeled with different masses (m1 < m2). Animate equal force arrows pushing both squares, with the smaller mass square accelerating faster (longer motion arrow) and the larger mass square accelerating slower (shorter motion arrow).

scenes.concept: Third Law - Law of Action and Reaction
scenes.visual: Show two circles facing each other labeled 'Object A' and 'Object B'. Animate arrows from Object A to Object B and from Object B to Object A, equal in length but opposite in direction, labeled 'Action Force' and 'Reaction Force'.
Show all three laws' key points as text bullet points on screen with simple icons: a stationary/moving object for the first law, formula F=ma for the second, and two opposing arrows for the third.
